front: Artist: member of Group f/64; best known for extreme close-up photographs of flowers, particularly magnolias (Magnolia Blossom, Two Callas)
Artist: appears in a Judy Dater photograph at Yosemite peeking at nude model Twinka Thiebaud behind a tree; the image parodies Thomas Hart Benton's painting Persephone
Artist: co-founded Group f/64 with Ansel Adams, Edward Weston, Willard Van Dyke, and Sonya Noskowiak
Photograph: 1931 image of dancer Martha Graham pressing both hands to her head/face
Artist: prints of her work feature Chinese stamps phonetically spelling her name, translating as 'Ideas Without End'

Artist: argued for spontaneity in street photography by pointing out that if Cartier-Bresson had asked the subject of Behind the Gare Saint-Lazare to step over the puddle again, the decisive moment would be ruined
Photograph: close-up of a nude woman's body forming the title shapes
